status_detail: Pilot scale hydrogen-based steelmaking in Lulea, Sweden. SSAB-LKAB-Vattenfall HYBRIT consortium. First fossil-free steel produced 2021. Includes 4.5 MW PEM electrolysis (Nel Hydrogen) for H2-DRI demonstration. Demonstrating complete fossil-free steel value chain from mine to steel. Full-scale industrial plant planned for 2026-2030.

data_management.narrative_summary: HYBRIT (Hydrogen Breakthrough Ironmaking Technology) is a pioneering fossil-free steel project in Sweden, led by SSAB, LKAB, and Vattenfall. The pilot plant in Lulea includes a 4.5 MW PEM electrolyzer from Nel Hydrogen that produces hydrogen for direct reduction of iron ore in a DRI shaft furnace. The project demonstrated the world's first fossil-free steel in 2021. The pilot has validated the complete value chain from iron ore to fossil-free steel using green hydrogen. A demonstration plant is under construction and a full-scale industrial plant is planned. This is the most advanced hydrogen-based steelmaking project globally and has been validated by independent lifecycle assessments showing near-zero CO2 emissions.

sources.title: SSAB HYBRIT — world-first fossil-free steel produced with hydrogen, August 2021
sources.author: SSAB / LKAB / Vattenfall
